Hey, what's up JB is in the house for your weekly intensive. It's gonna be a great one great topic Wow, you know for those you want certification, you know we're getting close to the end of the formal Training module by module right we're on looping and if you probably notice that I released half bit It's a really extra long module you'll be getting the other half tonight or first thing in the morning Depending on me. It's crazy So I think you guys are seeing that but it's a great lesson for me to really just kind of freeform one right now and kind of give you some thoughts of you know non-traditional ways of explaining looping because you know in the Training the formal straight line certification. I can't possibly explain it better More intricately and more organized than I did there. It's just I mean literally, you know, I spent probably I want to say six months on the looping module going back and forth Really that was like the one that was the the the the piece to resist thoughts And also that was the culmination of using the book I wrote the way of the wolf as the template and that one chapter took me 60 days. So it's like really I mean so you have with the certifications incredibly Logical step-by-step way Of understanding the process of looping. So let me now go the other way. I want to take you on the informal side I really want to Encourage you to ask questions, especially with this module because one of the things I promised all of you guys On certification said listen looping applies to any industry any business doesn't matter whether you're selling in person over the phone offline online It doesn't matter looping is universal It's a mindset a methodology of how you go about handling people who hit you with the common objections, right? And I said if you are having trouble using that, you know, the pre-established patterns They're just guidelines. You have to be flexible, right? So I want to really make sure all your questions get answered I'm going to save a little more I'm going to kind of cut the formal training a bit short and really go on the q&a for Hopefully 45 I'll go for an hour on q&a if you want as long as you keep asking me questions I'll keep going because I think it's that important. All right. Um, so let me start with this informal way of Explained looping Imagine you're Selling cars automobiles, right? You're an automobile salesman, right? And someone comes in and you know, they have basic knowledge of the car or they want to buy right? You go through a presentation, right? And then, you know, you formally ask them for the order tell them how Martial course how they get started be very simple you do the paperwork, right? And then they say let me think about it, right? Or let me call you back or I'm on a shop. I'm still shopping around, right? One of the common objections, right? If you were to say To a client, well, let me ask you a question, you know, I hear what you're saying bill But does the car make sense to you or do you like the car? Right? They'd look at you like had three heads on right That's not what looping is It's just not what all looping is It's this idea. It's a mindset a discipline that relates to the fact That objections by their very nature are smoke screens for uncertainty That's it. It's it's it's all looping just understand the mindset It's based on the fact that objections are smoke screens for uncertainty And in fact in the cert training that you haven't gotten this lesson it's less than 11 Was it 9 9 or 11? I go through every single common objection and I show you How at the end of the day what it really is it's a smoke screen for some type of uncertainty Even if it's two levels down that no matter how you slice it If you really start thinking an objection through like, you know, and why is that and why do you feel that way? You're always gonna get up because I'm not sure I'm not certain same thing, right? About one of the three tens always comes back there. So Looping represents this monumental breakthrough. I had 30 years ago That when I watched all my sales kids these kids right or kids, right? And when they would get stuck In the back half of the sale They would tend to go into this back and forth of Getting hit with an objection Trying to overcome that objection and then try to ask for the order again And it never worked doesn't work yet I realized that that that magic moment that night when I invented the straight line and everything's sort of coming clear to me I was like, wait, wait a second. That's not what I do When I get hit with an objection Yeah, I'll always answer the objection except for the first one I deflect for other reasons, right? But the poison i'm in that objection handling phase. Yeah I had the greatest answers the greatest rebuttals to every single objection But never once never once Perhaps I love to speak so much. So but never once would I just answer objection and they go for the close It would be like in my own mind. It was like an impossibility. Why would I do that? Because I know the objection tells me They still need to hear more. They have to learn more. They have to become more certain So when I answer an objection All it's doing Is it's basically earning me the right to continue the conversation You know, they say you want to think about and I say I understand you want to think about it But you know, I'm just just one that you would use I'm doing this for a long time And you know, you're a busy guy. I'm a busy guy. I know you'll put it in the back of your mind You'll end up deciding against this not because you don't love the idea because I know you do And not because you don't need this desk because I know you need to you're just busy You'll go back to your busy life. I'll go back to mine. We'll be like two shifts past the end of night and you'll miss out I don't want that to happen That's an answer to let me think about you know what I say I want to think about well I understand that but it's just like I've been doing this for a while pal Like what I'm saying is like I'm doing this and you know, every time someone says they want to think about it You know what I never hear from again And the sad part is is they they really need what I have. I know they could use it It'll improve their lives and they know it too But we're busy with busy people assuming beings right the best time To make a decision honestly is when all the facts are fresher in your mind and right now That's what's going right now. All the facts are fresher in your mind now Which one I use of those those one is like all the you know, listen honestly You want to think about right now? You just say some do this for quite some time and one thing I've learned is that when it comes to x y z You know the best decisions are made when all the facts are right in front of you and fresher your mind In fact, let me say this And in fact, let me say this what that is is that you're always your segue Into what really matters is building certainty There's there's no answer to an objection is over a bottle That's gonna make them want to buy from you. You have to get this through your head It's the most critical thing when it comes to people who are sitting on the fence and I and I Let me just get up and do my beautiful artistry. Okay here. Here's how certain I have to be All things being equal That all things being equals a very important statement. I just made how and I'm gonna tell you why I said so how Certain I have to be all things being equal before I'll say yeah, I'm comfortable enough to buy So again, someone like me my all things being equal meaning, you know I don't have to be that certain if I kind of kind of like something. I'm like, yeah, you know what I like to buy Stop I like making quick decisions. I've always done well like that. I I trust my instincts I trust my gut. I don't think people are out to screw me I'm a half. I'm a glass half full sort of guy my belief systems, right? Well, my father mad max ultra high action threshold He's the opposite You know, you have to be damn certain my father like damn certain No seeds of doubt or this guy is just he ain't buying My dad as you know is sick, right? And he's hanging in there because like I think he's like once he was too angry to die It is from like he's got too much pent up and angst the grim reaper is scared of me still hanging on he's actually even proven Which is awesome, right? I saw him last week. But you know, my dad's tough tough guy I'm a pushover compared to my dad when it comes to things like, you know, making decisions Negotiation I'm a very I'm an expert negotiator, but I come to the table to be friends I'm like, you know looking at you maddie in a pretty much positive light my dad Who's out to screw me different belief systems, right? And that that's the essence of action So all things being equal, right? If I kind of like something I'll buy it All things be cool. My dad if you like something He won't buy it, right? All things being equal. What's that mean? The absence of pain emergencies Bad things happening. You see that all things being equal means in the absence of some event In the absence of an urgency where I gotta do something because I'm not either I'm pain or I'm worried or uncle In the once that enters the equation all bets are off I don't care who you are how tough a buyer you are if you're feeling immediate massive pain over something You will take action to resolve that pain your action threshold temporarily drops through the floor And you buy to try to get out of pain and put yourself back in a feeling of not pleasure at least a feeling of being okay again We don't want to walk around feeling like crap. That's not how human beings are built Now How do we take all this so I kind of I gave you these five things really there was five points in there, right? I said what they got to be certain about the product and the little combined logical emotion to one thing, right? Certain about you the sales person said about the company also three things are three tens right basic stuff, right? I then said the beliefs that you have this sort of half Full half empty glass Who are you dealing with? What are their beliefs about buying? What are the beliefs about making decisions? What are their beliefs about? bearing risk What are their beliefs about Generally like you know am I capable Of making a good decision on my own do I need others to advise me like there's all these deep-seated beliefs That that are Engrained in us they've been formed Since we were very small by things that our parents said By things that we experienced ourselves things people said to us in passing Things we saw on tv Things we heard out of the corner of our ear And most importantly things that happened to us when they impacted us in a very painful way Those things formed very very strong beliefs and and they are usually limiting beliefs Like you know when you make that decision It's like in that in that moment when you're feeling bad And it hits you like oh, that's that's how limiting beliefs get formed You know like with me with not taking action to start my business when I was 23 I delayed starting strattin at first because of that one failure in the meat business one failure After eight or nine successes and as an entrepreneur when I was a kid onto 22 years old I had eight even more businesses One failure and I literally said myself. So I'm hovering around this great truth, right? And at the end of the day this great truth is like if at first you don't succeed Try try again. That's what looping is I sold you I gave I gave it to college straw. You said let me think about it I'm gonna sell you again, and I'm gonna sell you again now The problem is is if you try to do that Just do it you piss people off You break rapport with them You're pushing them away So we can't do that And of course I realized that as a natural born closer it occurred to me. I was like, all right Well, I know I gotta resell them. They're not sold enough yet. They're just not they're not sold enough They still need to be sold They're not sold But I just can't go out and sell them because it'll be pissed at me It's like I'm it's a confrontation. They say let me think about it. Let me just sell you again Hey, it doesn't work that way So looping is this sort of dance that you do with the prospect That allows you to keep reselling him again or again and again and again without pissing them off without breaking rapport Without them feeling that they've become disempowered. They're your punching bag. Like you're not hearing me That you're not gonna get very fall like that. Like, you know, they say let me think about you're like, well, listen, let me just tell you No, you you can't just keep going That's why I'm so this, you know, there's a lot of sales programs out there And and and and by the they're not all bad Some of these some of them, right? They're okay. Some of them Nothing's great, but there's some that are okay But it's interesting. They like there's some decent features yet when it comes to this thing They all miss it. They missed the boat on it. It's like they don't understand the the the toxic nature of a confrontation with your client Or your prospect you can't get into a test of wills with someone This is like there's an overriding factor that yes, I have to resell them I gotta make them more certain But if at any point in time I reveal myself to be a novice Or I bore them Or I come off as a hungry salesperson just wants commission Or they don't care I break break rapport Any one of those things happens like It's like death switch. It's not and by the way, it's not this is not death from a thousand cuts One cut you're dead These are knockout blows You break rapport or you really break reports and knockout blow you're not going to recover from that in the back end of the sale if if you are perceived In the looping section as someone you're just doing because you want to make money You're not going to close the person You're not there's not and any amount of loops in the world that can offset that positioning We must be perceived As someone who cares Who's on their side who wants to help them get what they want and by the way, this better be true I'm not like trying to say this in an evil way. This is what you're doing. This is what ethical persuasion is about You're helping someone resolve a problem You're helping them cure a pain point nothing wrong with that so Question Given I think I've kind of really beaten this dead horse as much as I can right now But I think you get it right you get what's going on looping is is just simply a methodology That allows you to keep reselling someone without pissing them off That's lame in terms to keep selling without pissing people off for as long as it takes until they buy Or the conversation ends and I get into that in lesson 11 or 12 right well 10 11 12 right okay so Let's talk about now How you would do that again? All right. The first one's from Donna Cundy Here's my questions for jb. I don't know if it's been discussed yet But is there a way to use the straight line when selling from stage? So a third loop Would be and again, so there's no okay So, you know, it's not set in stone, right? But typically your your first Pattern right you ask for the order And the prospect says let me think about it Forget hit with the first objection, right? So you deflect that first objection you move back into presentation mode You resell the product and you run your forest gun pattern Resell yourself the company that stands behind the product then you ask for the order again Now that's not to say that you can't you could also lower the action threshold as well You could do it in your first pad. It depends what you're selling what the circumstances if the person's in a rush Which is how you feel so there's no it's not set remember flexibility is king here The idea is that your ultimate. Let's look at your ultimate loop Your ultimate loop would be where you loop back you answer you so you always get a deflect the first objection, right? Let's say you're on your second objection your ultimate kitchen sink loop you loop back You resell the product logically and emotionally You resell yourself Logically and emotionally you resell the company that stands behind that lot product logically emotionally You then lower their action threshold and then you add on pain And then you take him into a soft close Obeying the energy in benefits out equation and you ask for the order that's your kitchen sink loop, right? You have everything in there. You have pain addition action threshold lowering pattern three tens logically and emotionally In the real world what we try to do is we you know, we try to be elegant and not just dump things So we'll I'll hold hardest Elements four and five I'm not gonna lower action threshold until I firstly let me just see if I can you know many times I found and this experience I have just that simply one basic loop of you know Making them more certain about the product running my forest gum pattern Transitioning into the company. I found that honestly 25 percent of the time or more even I'll close them right there with that one pattern Boom just like that now necessary if ever if it calls for if it's allowed meaning the product selling allows me to I'll engage in a step down sale as well Meaning if I start off I'll ask for 10,000 of whatever it is and then I'll say you want your stuff small at five That's how I could also step down So what do you do your third loop?
